demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents; totally dependent for all daily activities

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Please help — my 6-year-old daughter is breathing incredibly fast, then locking her breath until her lips turn blue and she almost passes out. It keeps happening over and over and we can't get her to stop.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It started about two hours ago after her evening routine got disrupted and she got really upset — she keeps holding her breath and going blue, then gasping, and we're terrified she's going to collapse or have a fit.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 2 hours ago, this evening, after a change to her usual routine upset her
symptom_narrative.duration: unrelenting for the last 2 hours — it won't settle
symptom_narrative.character: she breathes really fast and hard for a while, then holds her breath and stops breathing for up to about 45 seconds until her lips go blue and she nearly passes out, then she gasps and starts breathing fast again — round and round
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst we've ever seen it — she's exhausted and floppy after each one and we're scared she'll stop breathing or have a seizure
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: her breathing and her whole body — she goes stiff then floppy
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: cycles that keep repeating, one after another, without a proper break
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing we've tried tonight; sometimes being calm and quiet helps her a bit normally
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: she got much worse when she was upset, and the room is bright with the TV on loud
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: she's very agitated and won't stop wringing and washing her hands; she can't tell us what's wrong because she doesn't speak
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: she has these breathing spells sometimes but never this severe or this long — usually they settle on their own
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: she normally walks with a wide unsteady gait and needs help with everything; right now she can't be settled at all

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: She has Rett syndrome — she was developing normally as a baby and toddler and then lost her words and her hand skills.
past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: diagnosed as a toddler after she regressed
past_medical_history.how_managed: under a complex paediatric neurology team; managed with therapy and carer support

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: She doesn't speak and can't use her hands purposefully — she wrings and washes them constantly.
past_medical_history.how_managed: totally dependent for all care; non-verbal

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: Her head is small for her age and she walks with an unsteady wide gait.

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: 6-year-old cared for full-time at home by both parents, who are exhausted and very distressed tonight; she is non-verbal and cannot describe her symptoms.

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: known early-normal development followed by regression, with loss of speech and purposeful hand use; carer-reported throughout

objective_data_offered.value: when she holds her breath her lips and the skin around her mouth turn blue, then she gasps and breathes very fast again
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent describing what they can see on video; colour change is intermittent and hard to grade over a chat

objective_data_offered.value: she stops breathing for up to about 45 seconds each spell, goes floppy and nearly passes out, then it repeats — this has gone on for 2 hours
she is highly agitated and won't stop the hand-wringing and hand-washing movements
the room is brightly lit and the TV is on loud, and she seems more distressed with the noise and light
